1.dv: perhaps from an unused root meaning to stand out, or exist; entity; used adverbially or as a copula for the substantive verb (H1961 (הָיָה));

1.gl: to call out to (i.e. properly, address by name, but used in a wide variety of applications)

1.dv: a primitive root (rather identical with H7122 (קָרָא) through the idea of accosting a person met);

1.dv: an interrogative pronoun of persons, as H4100 (מָה) is of things,

1.gl: to turn; by implication, to face, i.e. appear, look, etc.

1.gl: 'I pray', 'now', or 'then'; added mostly to verbs (in the Imperative or Future), or to interjections, occasionally to an adverb or conjunction

1.dv: a primitive particle of incitement and entreaty, which may usually be rendered;

destroy, out of hand, kill, murder(-er), put to (death), make (slaughter), slay(-er), [idiom] surely.
anger, angry, grief, indignation, provocation, provoking, [idiom] sore, sorrow, spite, wrath.
